narrative:
Granit Xhaka is a crucial defensive midfielder for Switzerland at the World Cup 2026. His overall rating of 7.32/10 places him in the top 1% of defensive midfields at this tournament. This strong rating is significantly weighted by his club form over the last two seasons, which contributes 67% of the total.

Xhaka has shown a surprising offensive edge at the World Cup, scoring 0.188 goals per 90 minutes, a notable increase from his 0.046 goals per 90 minutes at club level. Defensively, he contributes with 0.938 tackles and 0.75 interceptions per 90 minutes over his 480 minutes played. His expected assists of 0.063 per 90 minutes, however, indicates a more limited creative output in the final third.

Xhaka clearly plays a dual role for his national team, anchoring the midfield defensively while also providing an unexpected goal threat. Scouts and fans should continue to monitor his offensive output, as his World Cup goal rate currently exceeds his expected goals of 0.176 per 90 minutes. His capacity to combine defensive stability with direct attacking contributions will be key in their remaining matches.
